title: What audit is checking after the page works
description: A short explanation of the audit pass across responsive behavior, accessibility, and anti-patterns.
content: audit checks whether the page is robust and trustworthy across responsiveness, accessibility, and obvious quality risks.

title: Why critique happens before polish
description: A short explanation of why the workflow challenges structure before refining details.
content: critique catches conceptual and structural weaknesses before time is spent polishing the wrong shape.

title: Why frontend-design matters in this workflow
description: A short explanation of why the workflow pushes against generic AI-looking layouts.
content: frontend-design exists to keep new pages intentional, distinctive, and aligned with the product’s real visual direction.

title: Why one issue, one branch, and one PR matters
description: A guide to the branch discipline behind the workflow and why it keeps delivery easier to reason about.
content: A branch tied to one execution issue keeps code, proof, and merge history easier to inspect.

title: What polish means before shipping
description: A short explanation of the final quality pass that tightens copy, spacing, and interaction details.
content: polish is the final pass that makes the page feel considered, not merely functional.

title: How write-a-prd, prd-to-plan, and prd-to-issues fit together
description: A plain-language explanation of why the workflow splits requirements, planning, and execution into separate steps.
content: The workflow separates idea, plan, and execution issue so each stage stays readable and auditable.

title: What proof means: design-proof vs acceptance-proof
description: Why this workflow requires visible evidence, not just a passing test run.
content: design-proof checks whether the page looks coherent, while acceptance-proof checks whether the real user journey works and can be inspected.

title: Why repo-bootstrap exists
description: A guide to the first skill in the workflow and why a repo should not begin life as an undocumented blank folder.
content: repo-bootstrap sets the rules, commands, docs, and proof bar before feature work starts.

title: Why shadcn-ui shows up in the build lane
description: A short explanation of why the workflow prefers dependable primitives before inventing custom controls.
content: shadcn-ui reduces risk in the primitive layer so design energy can go into composition, content, and clarity.

title: What agent skills are
description: A plain-language guide to what skills do, why they exist, and how they make an AI workflow more teachable.
content: Skills are reusable instruction packs that let the workflow stay consistent instead of depending on one-off chat context.

title: Choice Compass
description: A weighted decision helper prototype built through the full workflow chain.
content: A weighted decision helper prototype built through the full workflow chain. choice-compass Decision helper

title: OpenRouter Model Workbook Maintainer V2
description: A deterministic model-intelligence pipeline and guide that joins OpenRouter pricing with Artificial Analysis coverage and benchmark enrichment.
content: A deterministic model-intelligence pipeline and guide that joins OpenRouter pricing with Artificial Analysis coverage and benchmark enrichment. openrouter-model-workbook-maintainer-v2 Model intelligence guide

title: Proof Pack
description: A public project centered on visible evidence, browser proof, and inspectable output.
content: A public project centered on visible evidence, browser proof, and inspectable output. proof-pack Proof workflow

title: Property Search
description: A larger project where workflow rules, documentation, and tracked slices matter because the system itself is more complex.
content: A larger project where workflow rules, documentation, and tracked slices matter because the system itself is more complex. property-search Product system

title: Workflow Garden
description: A public educational site that turns an issue-driven AI workflow into something a visitor can understand.
content: A public educational site that turns an issue-driven AI workflow into something a visitor can understand. workflow-garden Educational site

title: What an issue branch is
description: A plain-language definition of a ticket-derived branch.
content: An issue branch is a branch named from the active execution ticket so the code and tracking stay aligned. ticket branch ticket-derived branch

title: What a PRD is
description: A plain-language definition of a PRD and why this workflow uses one.
content: A PRD is the clearest version of the product intent before implementation starts. product requirements document

title: What proof means in this workflow
description: A definition of proof as visible evidence rather than a polite claim that the change probably works.
content: Proof is inspectable evidence that a change works and reads well for another person, not just the implementer. proof artifacts proof-backed

content: Openrouter Model Workbook Maintainer V2 recorded Vercel hardening proof, masked workflow secrets immediately after Infisical fetch, and removed the deprecated Infisical action from the daily refresh. title: Public vote summary exposed, mobile scoreboard interactions hardened

content: Hackathon Voting Prototype exposed a public vote summary, removed scoreboard initial tiles, hardened mobile scoreboard interactions, and redirected proof tickets when a session already exists. content: Multi Agent Orchestration Demo redesigned the interface into a mission-control layout, retried slow generations on a faster model lane, and hardened the demo for public shipment. content: Openrouter Model Workbook Maintainer V2 relaxed rate-limit fallback matching, added a rate-limit fallback for daily refresh, made daily refresh fall back to repo secrets, and aligned local bootstrap messaging with Infisical. The environment contract, CI config, daily refresh workflow, and gitignore now document the changes, and the GitHub repo and latest commit link to the evidence. Seven commits touched 83 files, including the environment contract, CI config, daily refresh workflow, gitignore, agent task instructions, and Makefile. The team relaxed rate-limit fallback matching so the workflow survives transient API errors, then made daily refresh fall back to repo secrets when Infisical is unavailable. Operators can now trace fallback logic from rate-limit detection to repo secret usage in the public workflow, and the aligned bootstrap messaging removes confusion about when Infisical is required. Visit the OpenRouter Model Workbook Maintainer V2 project page to see how deterministic pipelines join pricing, coverage, and benchmark data.
